name: publish_connector_to_github
description: Publish a connector (Python/TS code, metadata, docs, tests, Dockerfile) from local path or generated dir to the grok-super-taskmaster (or any) GitHub repository using ONLY the desired effect: connector identifier + target repo + branch. No manual git, no multiple create calls needed. Internally uses the real grok_com_github__push_files (or create_or_update_file in loop) to atomically commit multiple files under connectors/ or connectors/<name>/ . Automatically handles new vs update via content, generates commit message. Ties directly to Connector Forge output for end-to-end 'forge then publish'.

connector_name.description: Name of connector to publish, e.g. 'taskmaster-mcp' or 'my-new-api-connector'. Used to locate local files or generated dir.

source_dir.description: Optional explicit local source dir containing the connector files to publish (e.g. workdir/taskmaster-mcp or Connector_Forge/generated_connectors/foo)

file_paths.description: Optional explicit list of relative file paths to include; if omitted, auto-discovers common connector files (server.py, Dockerfile, README.md, *.json, etc.)

steps.description: Resolve source_dir from param or standard locations (Connector_Forge/generated_*, workdir/*-mcp, .grok/bundled). Collect all relevant files: code, Dockerfiles, README, metadata, index updates.
Read file contents. Prefix paths under 'connectors/<connector_name>/' or 'mcps/<name>/' or root 'tasks/' for defs. Build array of {path, content} for push.

Call grok_com_github__push_files (preferred for multi-file atomic) or sequence of grok_com_github__create_or_update_file . Provide owner, repo, branch, files[], message='Publish connector <name> via TASK publish_connector_to_github'. Real GitHub write, not simulated.
Use grok_com_github__get_file_contents on the pushed paths or grok_com_github__search_code to confirm. Optionally create PR if branch protection.

symbiotic: Often follows forge_new_connector or after local edits to a podman MCP.
